name: hush
description: Token-lean sessions at the harness level, not the prompt level. Ships a forced output style that rewires the system prompt itself — no preamble, no play-by-play, one outcome-first final message at dev-shorthand density (default to fragments, not caveman-speak) — plus a PostToolUse hook that mechanically shrinks Bash/PowerShell output before it enters context (ANSI/progress-bar strip, duplicate collapse, line caps that always preserve warning/error/failure lines regardless of position; failures keep ~4x more, verbatim), and a dual-mode narration meter (PostToolUse + Stop) that injects one corrective line the moment mid-turn narration crosses budget — inside the offending turn, at most once per turn. Replaces prompt-injection style plugins: same terseness goal, stronger mechanism, and it also cuts the input-token side they never touch. Tune via HUSH_CAP_PASS, HUSH_CAP_FAIL, HUSH_NARRATION_BUDGET; disable the meter alone with HUSH_NARRATION=off, or everything with HUSH_DISABLE=1.
